summary: A two-story residential building with a flat roof topped by a brick parapet. The structure features a rectangular footprint with symmetrical window placement on both floors. Walls are constructed primarily from sandstone blocks with dark stone corner reinforcements. A concrete foundation platform elevates the building above ground level. The roof is finished with brick and concrete coping.
rebuild_hints: Build 12x12 block rectangular footprint at ground level
Construct 2-block-high concrete foundation platform surrounding the building
Create two floors of 3-block height each using sandstone blocks
Place dark stone blocks at all four corners as vertical reinforcement pillars
Install 4 window panes per floor in symmetrical arrangement (2x2 grid pattern)
Add horizontal wood plank trim between first and second floor
Top the building with 1-block-high brick parapet on all sides
Cap the roof with concrete blocks for coping
Ensure bilateral symmetry on all four sides
Use consistent window spacing: approximately 3 blocks apart horizontally
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and room configuration not visible
Precise block count for depth and width estimated from perspective views
Door location not clearly visible in any view
Exact wood material type for trim (could be oak, spruce, or other variants)
Whether roof has any interior structure or is purely decorative parapet
Exact shade variations of sandstone (may include multiple sandstone variants)
